spec_match.rationale: Surfaces the exact Use-As-Dictionary header with the new ttl=<seconds> parameter and shows the dictionary going from FRESH (age < ttl, browser sends Available-Dictionary) to EVICTED (age >= ttl, no Available-Dictionary), matching the chromestatus summary.

interactive.rationale: Three live controls (match pattern text input, ttl range, age range) drive a real header preview, request preview, and state/expiry readout that update on each input event.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb promises 'dial a TTL and elapsed time, see the Use-As-Dictionary response, see whether browser still has the dictionary' — page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers fresh vs evicted. Missing: ttl=0 (immediately-expires) behaviour, interaction with Cache-Control max-age on the dictionary itself (which is independent), missing-ttl fallback to the default (which the summary calls out), and the match-dest / match-type axes the full Use-As-Dictionary spec carries.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/6408307086589952 but omits the compression-dictionary spec at datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-httpbis-compression-dictionary — the sibling concept variant-explosion links to it directly.

design_system.rationale: Uses --bg-*, --text-*, --accent-emerald (fresh) and --accent-rose (stale) via CSS variables; no static-card pattern; range inputs accent-color set to --text-black.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate ttl=0 (immediately-expires) corner case
openQuestions.detail: ttl=0 is the corner case implementers care about — it means 'don't allow this dictionary on subsequent requests' which is functionally different from omitting Use-As-Dictionary entirely. The age-slider currently starts at 0, but the ttl slider's floor is 60 so the two-zero case can't be reached. Lower the ttl floor and add a tooltip explaining the spec behaviour.

openQuestions.title: Add the IETF spec link to references
openQuestions.detail: Sibling concept variant-explosion links https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-httpbis-compression-dictionary/ — this concept should too. Improves consistency across the feature and gives readers the normative source.

openQuestions.title: Show the interaction with Cache-Control max-age
openQuestions.detail: Use-As-Dictionary ttl bounds dictionary usefulness; Cache-Control max-age on the same response bounds whether the dictionary file itself stays in the HTTP cache. These are independent and developers conflate them. The current header preview shows Cache-Control: max-age=3600 but never explains that this lifetime is separate from ttl. Add a second slider and a 'when each expires' annotation.

summary: Clean header-level explainer that nails the headline behaviour. To go deeper, demo ttl=0, show ttl-vs-Cache-Control independence, and add the IETF draft to references for parity with the sibling pages.
